title: Podman dies during interactive session exec due to disconnect
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
When running a shell script in the interactive shell with the terminal during exec , podman dies if session is disconnected.
 
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
This is just an example, you can 
1.  Connect to your remote host via ssh.  Run bash in the podman e.g.
podman exec -it  containerx /bin/bash

2.  Run loop e.g.
while x=1; do  ls; sleep 1; done

3.  Disconnect ssh session

**Describe the results you received:**
Container dies.     
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la sshd[5785]: Received disconnect from 10.10.8.142 port 33360:11: disconnected by user
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la sshd[5785]: Disconnected from user root 10.10.8.142 port 33360
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la sshd[5785]: pam_unix(sshd:session): session closed for user root
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la ceph-mon-storage2n1-la[6614]: teardown: managing teardown after SIGCHLD
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la ceph-mon-storage2n1-la[6614]: teardown: Sending SIGTERM to PID 173
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la ceph-mon-storage2n1-la[6614]: debug 2019-05-29 07:58:26.956 7f393d287700 -1 received  signal: Terminated from  (PID: 259) UID: 0
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la ceph-mon-storage2n1-la[6614]: debug 2019-05-29 07:58:26.956 7f393d287700 -1 mon.storage2n1-la@0(leader) e1 *** Got Signal Terminated ***
May 29 07:58:26 storage2n1-la systemd-logind[1256]: Removed session 524.
-- Subject: Session 524 has been terminated

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container stays alive with and bash session dies.   This works if I just do exec /bin/bash and don't run loop in that shell.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
There is an additional problem.  The session dies uncleanly.  Systemd cannot restart it unless I do podman rm --force <container name>

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.3.2-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.4
  podman version: 1.3.2-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0-dev
  Conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/crio/conmon
    version: 'conmon version , commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  MemFree: 117319438336
  MemTotal: 134647275520
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 6446641152
  SwapTotal: 6446641152
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 40
  hostname: storage2n1-la
  kernel: 4.18.0-20-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 163h 34m 28.88s (Approximately 6.79 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 8
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical host

comments.body: how have you created the container?

ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --rm --name ceph-mon-%i \
  --log-opt path=/dev/null \
  --memory=128409m \
--cpus=1 \
-v /var/lib/ceph:/var/lib/ceph:z \
  -v /etc/ceph:/etc/ceph:z \
  -v /var/run/ceph:/var/run/ceph:z \
  -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro \
--net=host \
-e IP_VERSION=4 \
  -e MON_IP=10.0.40.121 \
  -e CLUSTER=ceph \
  -e FSID=9b4468b7-5bf2-4964-8aec-4b2f4bee87ad \
  -e MON_PORT=3300 \
  -e CEPH_PUBLIC_NETWORK=10.0.40.0/23 \
  -e CEPH_DAEMON=MON \
  -e CONTAINER_IMAGE=docker.xcastlabs.net/ceph/daemon:latest-nautilus-centos-7-x86_64 \
   \
  docker.xcastlabs.net/ceph/daemon:latest-nautilus-centos-7-x86_64
It is a ceph container
@alitvak69 Are you running as root, or rootless?
I'm wondering if this might be systemd killing the cgroup Podman is in.
@mheon I am running as root
Hmm. Is this exec only? The container in question keeps running, but the exec session dies?
I *think* the conmon exec work from @haircommander - #3143 - will solve this one
Container dies.  If exec would die I wouldn't complain.  Container dies not cleanly and I have to force remove it to start a new one.
how are you disconnecting ssh session?
(just wondering so I can test my patch to see if it does fix it like @mheon thinks)
I believe it is reproducible with a similarly run container of any sort.  It doesn't have to be ceph.
I have a terminal open with Xshell (could be any terminal software I guess)  and I click disconnect.
Under linux / unix I beleive you could just close a terminal window.

I was unable to reproduce with
`podman run -d alpine top`
`podman exec -til /bin/sh`
`while x=1; do ls; sleep 1; done`
**disconnect**

@alitvak69 are you able to find a reproducer that is less complicated than the ceph container? otherwise, I can look into it more tomorrow. I feel similarly that the patch will at least not brick the container if the exec session is disconnected
You are detaching container (-d) and I do not,
Also, I run container via systemd unit perhaps this has anything to do with the issue?
Maybe running some web server with --rm without -d  will help.   I guess I will try to find something later today,

@alitvak69 oh yes, I got it
1: `podman run alpine top` 
2: `podman exec -til /bin/sh`
2: `while x=1; do ls; sleep 1; done`
**kill 2**
**kill 1**
`podman rm -l`
`Error: cannot remove container ffde6a6bf513b7bebe8d2480c46db953a711987da2a52570f05a65c22e57c9d5 as it has active exec sessions: container state improper
`

The patch mentioned above doesn't fix, but it shouldn't be a bad follow up PR to fix this issue.
@haircommander is this fixed?

@baude @alitvak69 it is not fixed, but I'm actually not convinced it's a problem anymore. the behavior of having a running exec session in a container is similar to the container's main process running. Right now, to remove a running container, -f is needed, and it is similarly needed for a to remove a container with a running exec process.

@haircommander I think it is a problem because exec is not killed by choice.  Yes we do it to reproduce the problem but exec is also dying if my ssh session is disconnected due to a connection failure for example

@haircommander I understand that you need to cleanup with -f sometimes but I don't understand why running a bash script in container exec shell should cause the  whole thing to die during a terminal session disconnection.  I believe it should cause a script itself to die as well as exec session but not the running container.  Anyway, what would be a comparable docker or rkt behavior?  I used both and don't recall either had the same issue.
I agree an exec session dieing should not cause the container to exit, just hte exec session.

@alitvak69 can you test master with your set up? given your description and @rhatdan's comment, this is actually fixed:
```1: podman run alpine top
2: podman exec -til /bin/sh
2: while x=1; do ls; sleep 1; done
disconnect 2
disconnect 1
```
allows me to leave the top session running
@haircommander What released version of podman I need to run in order to verify the fix?
@alitvak69 We don't have a release including it yet, but we're in the process of cutting 1.5.0 which will include it - down to two PRs before it's done, so hopefully tomorrow.
@haircommander  @mheon I am out this week so I will try testing it early next week and report it back to you.

The box I could get for testing was a centos instance I was tasked with testing latest podman on centos in addition. :-)  Problem is the centos rpm 1.5.0.2 on CBS failed to build, and my attempt to use the f30 and cri-o branches on https://github.com/lsm5/rpm-builder/ failed due to my lack of understanding of how to get the environment ready.  Can any of you either look into 1.5.0.2 on CBS or better yet give me a hint on how to use git repo mentioned above to build those RPMs, this way I don't have to wait for new package to drop or bother you with that and can do builds based on master and tagged release going forward.

Thank you,
I have tested with 1.5.1 and disconnecting ssh session while running the loop no longer kills the container.
@alitvak69 So we can close this issue?

Yes you can.  Thank you.
